content_markdown:
A loudspeaker emits sound of frequency $f$. The sound waves are reflected from a wall. The arrangement is shown below.

When a microphone is moved along the line SW, minimum loudness of sound is detected at points P, Q and R. There are no other minima between these points. The separation of the minima is $d$.

The speed of the sound wave is

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a loudspeaker S emitting sound towards a wall W. Points P, Q, and R are marked on the line SW between the speaker and the wall. The distance between P and Q is d, and the distance between Q and R is d.

vibe_explanation: The interference between the incident and reflected waves creates a standing wave pattern. In a standing wave, the distance between consecutive nodes (minima) is equal to half a wavelength ($\lambda/2$). Given that the distance between minima is $d$, we have $d = \lambda/2$, which means $\lambda = 2d$. Using the wave equation $v = f\lambda$, we substitute the wavelength to get $v = f(2d) = 2fd$.
